key_insight: Chemical disorder and local chemical order in WTaCrV change how irradiation damage forms, but both can better preserve strength than a homogenized average-atom model.
engineering_relevance: For reactor structural alloys, controlling nanoscale chemistry may be as important as composition for improving resistance to radiation damage and mechanical degradation.
method: Atomistic simulations compared three configurations: Average-Atom (AA), random solid-solution (RSS), and local chemical order (LCO).
New interatomic potentials were used to run collision cascade and deformation analyses.
Primary radiation defects and post-irradiation mechanical response were evaluated across the three models.
evidence_signal: Collision cascades showed Frenkel-pair production ranked NRSS > NLCO > NAA at the same dose, while irradiation strongly degraded the AA model but had limited impact on strength and flow stress in RSS and LCO.
watch_item: The conclusions depend on atomistic potentials and modeled nanoscale structures, so transferability to real irradiated microstructures and experimental conditions should be validated.
paper_title: Primary damage and mechanical degradation of WTaCrV refractory high-entropy alloy: effects of solid-solution and chemical ordering
